title: Llama 4 Scout vs Claude Haiku 4.5 in the Compare Prices Grid
description: Meta's budget Llama 4 Scout vs Anthropic's Haiku 4.5 in the Compare Prices grid — both fast-tier, very different price points.

sections.heading: Why a Side-by-Side Comparison of Llama 4 Scout and Claude Haiku 4.5 Matters
sections.body: The [Compare Prices tool](/tools/compare-prices) is the fastest way to put a shortlist of LLMs in a single grid — input cost, output cost, context window, and quality score in stacked columns you can scan vertically. Provider dropdowns let you mix models across Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, Meta, DeepSeek, Mistral, and xAI without leaving the page. For Llama 4 Scout vs Claude Haiku 4.5, the side-by-side framing matters because both models sit near the same workload niche — one of you ships the wrong pick and the bill (or quality regression) is months of pain. Llama 4 Scout runs $0.200 / $0.600 per 1M tokens with a 1M context and a blended quality score of 60. Claude Haiku 4.5 runs $1.00 / $5.00 per 1M with a 200K context and quality 65. Sticker prices don't tell the whole story — the Value column (quality ÷ input cost) gives Llama 4 Scout a 300 and Claude Haiku 4.5 a 65, which is the number you actually want to optimize when shipping production traffic. For the underlying math, see [tokens-to-dollars conversion](/blog/tokens-to-dollars-conversion); for routing strategy see [multi-model routing with quality scores](/blog/multi-model-routing-with-quality-scores).

sections.heading: Llama 4 Scout in the Compare Prices Grid
sections.body: In the [Compare Prices view](/tools/compare-prices), click the **Meta** dropdown and check **Llama 4 Scout**. The row shows input at $0.200/1M, output at $0.600/1M, 1M context, and the blended quality badge at 60. Llama 4 Scout sits in TokenRate's **fast** tier — fast tier is built for high-volume throughput at the lowest per-token rate the provider offers. The output-to-input ratio of 3.0x is worth flagging because generation-heavy workloads (long summaries, code, structured output) compound that multiplier across every reply. For a single-shot classifier the input price dominates; for an agent generating ~10× the tokens it reads, you're effectively paying $0.600 per 1M.

sections.heading: Claude Haiku 4.5 in the Compare Prices Grid
sections.body: Add **Claude Haiku 4.5** from the **Anthropic** dropdown. The grid lists input $1.00/1M, output $5.00/1M, 200K context, quality 65, tier **fast**. fast tier is built for high-volume throughput at the lowest per-token rate the provider offers. Compared to Llama 4 Scout, Claude Haiku 4.5 is pricier on input (by 400%) and higher on quality (by 5 points). Context-window-wise, Claude Haiku 4.5 has a tighter window — relevant if you're feeding long documents.

sections.heading: Where Llama 4 Scout Wins and Where Claude Haiku 4.5 Wins
sections.body: **Llama 4 Scout wins on raw cost** ($0.200 vs $1.00 input — about 5.0× cheaper) — so it's the right pick for high-volume features where the model is fungible across the chosen tier. **Claude Haiku 4.5 wins on quality** (65 vs 60) — important when you're routing reasoning-heavy or accuracy-critical traffic. **Llama 4 Scout wins on Value** (300 vs 65) — meaning per dollar of input you get more quality-adjusted output, which is what the [Value column](/blog/value-column-vs-tokens-per-dollar-metric) optimizes for. For long-context tasks, Llama 4 Scout's 1M window wins.

sections.heading: Decision Heuristics and What to Do Next
sections.body: Three heuristics: (1) if your monthly bill on the pricier option exceeds 4× your engineering team's comfort and the cheaper option's quality is within 5 points — ship the cheaper one and pocket the savings. (2) if the workload is reasoning-heavy or customer-facing premium, pay the quality premium even when the Value column says otherwise. (3) hedge: route 80–90% of traffic to the cheaper model and fall back to the pricier one for tail-quality cases. The fallback router pattern works because output-cost only matters when you actually call it. For the routing implementation, see [multi-model routing with quality scores](/blog/multi-model-routing-with-quality-scores). Pricing is pulled live from [OpenRouter's models endpoint](https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/models) and revalidated every 60 minutes via Next.js's incremental cache, so the grid you see is at most an hour stale. Quality scores blend [Arena AI](https://lmarena.ai/leaderboard) Elo with [Artificial Analysis](https://artificialanalysis.ai) intelligence-index data on the same cadence.

faq.question: How do I open the Compare Prices grid?
faq.answer: Two ways: click the 'Compare Prices' tab at the top of the calculator card on the home page, or navigate directly to /tools/compare-prices. The standalone page is also linked from the main navigation under 'Tools'.

faq.question: Can I share my comparison with teammates?
faq.answer: Yes — the page URL captures the current state. Send the link in Slack and your teammate sees the same grid. Useful for procurement and architecture-review meetings.

faq.question: Is the data live or cached?
faq.answer: Live from OpenRouter (prices) and a blended Arena AI + Artificial Analysis pipeline (quality), refreshed on a 60-minute incremental cache. So the grid is at most an hour stale.

faq.question: Where do I go after the grid to project monthly cost?
faq.answer: Once you've picked a winner, go to /tools/api-cost-estimator and plug in the model + your expected monthly token volume. The estimator does the per-1M math against your real workload mix.

ctaText: Try the comparison yourself at [/tools/compare-prices](/tools/compare-prices) — it's the fastest way to stack model cost, context, and quality in a single grid.
